meta.contract: Each question supplies fillers for every role its essay type's cards can request, in both shapes. 'np' = noun phrase (fits after of / about / against / in spite of). 'cl' = full clause with subject and verb (fits after that / although / despite the fact that). Arrays are paraphrase variants — the engine rotates through them so the topic is reworded, never repeated verbatim. AUTHORING RULES: (1) cl variants must NOT contain time adverbials (nowadays, these days, now) — the card hooks supply those. (2) np variants must read naturally after 'the pros and cons of ___'. (3) Every pro/con/reason/cause/solution/prediction needs whichever shape(s) its essayType's cards actually request — see rolesByType below.

rolesByType.for-against: topic {np,cl}, counter {np,cl}, pros[0]/pros[1] -> pro1/pro2 {cl only}, cons[0] -> con1 {np,cl}, verdict {np only, FA-3/5/6 only}
rolesByType.agree-disagree: topic {np,cl}, counter {cl only}, verdict {np,cl}, r1/r2/r3 {cl only}
rolesByType.opinion: topic {np,cl}, context {cl only}, r1/r2/r3 {cl only}, verdict {np,cl}

questions.mainQuestion: Is it better to earn a lot of money or to enjoy your job?

np: prioritising a high salary over job satisfaction
choosing money over enjoyment at work
putting earnings before a fulfilling career
cl: more and more young people are choosing well-paid jobs over work they truly enjoy
many school leavers put salary at the top of their list when choosing a career

np: the growing appeal of doing work one genuinely loves
the widespread belief that happiness at work matters more than money
cl: many people insist that enjoying one's job matters more than any salary
others feel that happiness at work is what truly counts

pros.np: the financial security a high salary provides
pros.cl: a high salary offers financial security and real freedom of choice

pros.np: the lifestyle that higher earnings make possible
pros.cl: well-paid workers can afford better housing, travel and further education
money worries are among the main causes of stress in adult life

cons.np: spending years in a job one dislikes
cons.cl: spending forty hours a week on work you dislike can damage both health and motivation

cons.np: the risk of burnout in an unloved career
cons.cl: no salary can fully compensate for daily boredom and frustration

np: a balanced recognition that money alone cannot buy a fulfilling working life
room to argue that satisfaction ultimately pays the higher dividend

questions.mainQuestion: Every country in the world has problems with pollution and damage to the environment. Do you think these problems can be solved?

np: pollution and damage to the environment
the growing problem of pollution across the world
cl: pollution and environmental damage have become a serious problem in almost every country
every country in the world is now grappling with pollution and damage to the environment

np: rapid industrialisation and the constant demand for cheap goods
keep up with rising demand for transport and consumer goods
cl: industries have long prioritised cheap production over protecting the environment
economic growth has often been pursued with little regard for the environment

sol1.cl: the best way to tackle this is that governments could invest heavily in renewable energy

sol2.np: placing stricter regulations on industrial waste
sol2.cl: another practical measure is that stricter regulations could be placed on industrial waste

sol3.np: making public transport cheaper and more widely available
sol3.cl: an additional solution would be that public transport could be made cheaper and more widely available

questions.mainQuestion: What are the causes and effects of social media addiction among young people?

np: social media addiction among young people
the growing problem of social media addiction
cl: many young people have become addicted to social media
social media addiction among young people is becoming increasingly common

np: the addictive design of social media platforms
the constant stream of notifications and rewards built into these apps
cl: social media platforms are deliberately designed to keep users engaged for as long as possible
these apps are built around features that reward users for staying online

c1.cl: one of the main causes is that constant notifications train users to check their phones compulsively

c2.cl: a further contributing factor is that comparing themselves to others online damages young people's self-esteem

c3.np: its damaging effect on mental health
c3.cl: another important cause is that heavy use is linked to rising anxiety and depression among teenagers

questions.mainQuestion: 'We should do everything we can to save animals which are in danger of disappearing from our planet.' Do you agree?

np: protecting animals in danger of extinction
saving endangered animals
cl: protecting endangered animals is one of the most pressing environmental issues today
many species are now in real danger of disappearing from our planet

counter.cl: conservation efforts can be extremely expensive and difficult to enforce

np: the responsibility to save endangered animals
an obligation to protect species at risk of extinction
cl: we should do everything possible to save animals in danger of disappearing
protecting endangered species should be treated as a global priority

r1.cl: what convinces me most is that many iconic species such as tigers and rhinos are close to extinction

r2.cl: a further reason I hold this view is that every species plays a vital role in maintaining ecological balance

r3.cl: an additional argument is that losing key species can disrupt entire food chains

questions.mainQuestion: 'Life is better today than it was 100 years ago.' Do you agree?

np: life today compared with life a hundred years ago
how much life has changed over the last century
cl: life today is far better than it was 100 years ago
life has improved enormously over the last hundred years

context.cl: healthcare, technology and equality have all advanced enormously over the last century

r1.cl: my first reason for thinking so is that healthcare has advanced enormously

r2.cl: an additional consideration is that entertainment options have expanded dramatically

r3.cl: I also feel strongly that technology has transformed how people communicate

np: the view that life today is better than it was a century ago
a strong case that today's world offers a far better standard of living
cl: life today is significantly better than it was a century ago
the last hundred years have brought genuine progress to people's everyday lives

questions.mainQuestion: Do you think technology will improve our lives in the future?

np: the effect technology will have on our lives in the future
the future impact of technology on everyday life
cl: technology will continue to transform our daily lives
technology is set to play an even bigger role in how we live

np: the rapid pace of advances in artificial intelligence and automation
ever-faster developments in computing power
cl: advances in artificial intelligence and automation are accelerating fast
new technologies are being developed and adopted faster than ever before

p1.cl: one strong possibility is that technology will make communicating across the world even easier

p2.cl: a further development may well be that automation will take over dangerous and repetitive jobs

p3.cl: there is every chance that artificial intelligence could revolutionise how diseases are diagnosed
